rivers.name: South Fork New River
rivers.description: One of the oldest rivers in the world. The South Fork flows through the NC High Country — gentle and scenic, perfect for tubing and canoeing.
rivers.put_in: New River State Park, Sparta NC

access_points.name: New River State Park Canoe Launch
access_points.description: Official canoe and kayak launch inside New River State Park. Ample parking and restrooms on site.
access_points.notes: Day use fee required. Open year-round.

access_points.name: Wagoner Access Swimming Hole
access_points.description: Popular shallow swimming and wading area with a sandy gravel bar. Great for families.
access_points.notes: No lifeguard on duty. Watch for current during high water.

access_points.name: Tessentee Creek Confluence Fishing
access_points.description: Productive smallmouth bass and musky fishing where Tessentee Creek meets the New River.
access_points.notes: NC fishing license required. Check NCWRC regulations for size limits.

rivers.name: New River
rivers.description: The main stem New River flows through the Virginia Blue Ridge near Galax. One of the oldest rivers on Earth, offering classic flatwater paddling, excellent smallmouth bass fishing, and scenic mountain views.

access_points.name: Cliffview Access Put-In
access_points.description: Primary put-in near Galax with access to the New River Trail corridor. Parking and restrooms available.
access_points.notes: New River Trail State Park. Day use fee applies. Shuttle service available seasonally.

access_points.name: Fries Junction Take-Out
access_points.description: Popular take-out at Fries Junction where the New River Trail crosses. Road access and parking on site.
access_points.notes: Roughly 7 miles from Cliffview. Active rail-trail — watch for cyclists.

access_points.name: Galax Riverside Fishing
access_points.description: Excellent smallmouth bass and musky fishing along the Galax section. Deep pools and rocky structure hold big fish.
access_points.notes: VA fishing license required. Check VDWR regulations for size and bag limits.

rivers.name: Watauga River
rivers.description: A beautiful mountain river flowing through Watauga County. Popular for trout fishing and kayaking with scenic gorge sections.
rivers.put_in: Sugar Grove Access, Sugar Grove NC

access_points.name: Sugar Grove Kayak Put-In
access_points.description: Main put-in for the popular Sugar Grove to Dugger Mine run. Roadside pullout with river access.
access_points.notes: Limited parking on shoulder of SR-1114. Arrive early on weekends.

access_points.name: Dugger Mine Take-Out
access_points.description: Standard take-out point for Sugar Grove run. Small gravel bar with road access.
access_points.notes: Park along the road, do not block gate. Roughly 4-mile run from Sugar Grove.

access_points.name: Valle Crucis Fishing Access
access_points.description: Excellent trophy trout fishing in the Valle Crucis section. Wild and stocked brown and rainbow trout.
access_points.notes: Catch-and-release only in designated trophy sections. Artificial lures only. NC fishing license required.

access_points.name: Laurel Creek Swimming Hole
access_points.description: Scenic swimming hole at Laurel Creek confluence. Cool, clear water with a small waterfall upstream.
access_points.notes: Slippery rocks — water shoes recommended. No facilities.

rivers.name: Nolichucky River
rivers.description: World-class whitewater flowing through a stunning gorge. Home to challenging Class III-IV rapids beloved by experienced paddlers.

access_points.name: Poplar Put-In
access_points.description: Primary put-in for the Nolichucky Gorge run. Small parking area near the railroad tracks.
access_points.notes: Active railroad — stay clear of tracks. Experienced paddlers only; Class IV at normal flows.

access_points.name: Erwin Take-Out (Chestoa)
access_points.description: Main take-out below the gorge at Chestoa Recreation Area in Erwin, TN. Large parking lot.
access_points.notes: Free parking. Restrooms available seasonally. About 8.5 miles from Poplar put-in.

access_points.name: Erwin Riverside Fishing
access_points.description: Good smallmouth bass and rainbow trout fishing along the Erwin riverside section below the gorge.
access_points.notes: TN fishing license required. Check TWRA regulations for stocked trout sections.

rivers.name: Wilson Creek
rivers.description: A wild and scenic river near Mortimer NC with technical whitewater. Flows through Wilson Creek Gorge with spectacular scenery.

access_points.name: Mortimer Campground Put-In
access_points.description: Put-in at the USFS Mortimer Campground. Access to upper Wilson Creek runs.
access_points.notes: Camping fee if staying overnight. Day use parking available. Class IV+ below this point.

access_points.name: Lower Wilson Creek Take-Out
access_points.description: Take-out at the lower bridge on SR-1328. Ends the classic Wilson Creek gorge run.
access_points.notes: Small pull-off, limited parking. Shuttle required — very remote area, plan ahead.

access_points.name: Wilson Creek Trophy Trout Access
access_points.description: Trophy trout section of Wilson Creek managed by NCWRC. Catch-and-release wild brown trout.
access_points.notes: Artificial lures only. NC fishing license required. Wading access from USFS road.

access_points.name: Schoolhouse Falls Swimming Hole
access_points.description: Stunning plunge pool at the base of Schoolhouse Falls. One of the most scenic swimming spots in the High Country.
access_points.notes: Short 0.6-mile hike from Burnthouse Branch Road parking area. Cold water year-round.

rivers.name: Elk Creek
rivers.description: A tributary of the Watauga near Elk Park, offering intimate paddling through mountain scenery. Good beginner creek runs when levels are right.
rivers.put_in: Elk Park Bridge, Elk Park NC

access_points.name: Elk Park Bridge Put-In
access_points.description: Put-in beneath the US-19E bridge in Elk Park. Easy river access on river left.
access_points.notes: Park on the gravel shoulder. Do not block traffic. Class II-III run to confluence with Watauga.

access_points.name: Elk Creek Trout Fishing
access_points.description: Excellent native brook trout and stocked rainbow trout fishing in the upper reaches of Elk Creek.
access_points.notes: NC fishing license required. Check NCWRC for stocked sections. Best in spring and fall.

access_points.name: Elk Creek Swimming Hole
access_points.description: Quiet swimming hole below a small ledge drop downstream of the Elk Park bridge.
access_points.notes: Shallow entry — check depth before jumping. No facilities nearby.

rivers.name: South Toe River
rivers.description: A crystalline mountain river running through the Black Mountains near Mount Mitchell. Cold, clear water ideal for fishing and light paddling.

access_points.name: Celo Community Put-In
access_points.description: Put-in near the Celo Community bridge on the South Toe. Good access for Class II-III run toward Burnsville.
access_points.notes: Please respect private property near the access. Park in designated pull-off only.

access_points.name: Burnsville Take-Out
access_points.description: Take-out at the Highway 19W bridge near Burnsville. Ends the lower South Toe run.
access_points.notes: Shuttle required. Roughly 9 miles from Celo put-in. Parking near bridge.

access_points.name: Black Mountain Campground Fishing
access_points.description: Prime native brook trout habitat in the headwaters near USFS Black Mountain Campground at the base of Mount Mitchell.
access_points.notes: Artificial lures only in designated areas. NC license required. Hike-in sections offer solitude.

access_points.name: South Toe Swimming Hole at Celo
access_points.description: Beautiful gravel-bar swimming hole with a deep pool. Crystal-clear cold water straight off the Black Mountains.
access_points.notes: Popular on summer weekends. Water is very cold even in August — ideal for a quick dip after hiking.

rivers.name: Nantahala River
rivers.description: One of the Southeast's most popular whitewater destinations. This dam-release gorge run offers consistent Class II-III paddling and culminates at the iconic Class IV Nantahala Falls just above the Nantahala Outdoor Center.

access_points.name: Patton's Run Put-In
access_points.description: Main put-in for the classic Nantahala gorge run. Roadside access off US-19 with ample parking.
access_points.notes: Most popular section is 8 miles to NOC. Outfitter shuttles available. Water is cold year-round — wetsuit recommended.

access_points.name: NOC Take-Out / Wesser
access_points.description: Take-out at the Nantahala Outdoor Center in Wesser. Ends directly below Nantahala Falls (Class IV). Full outfitter services on site.
access_points.notes: Nantahala Falls is Class IV — scout or portage if uncertain. NOC has rentals, shuttles, food, and lodging.

access_points.name: Nantahala Gorge Trout Fishing
access_points.description: Cold, clear tailwater fishery below Nantahala Lake dam. Excellent stocked and wild rainbow and brown trout throughout the gorge.
access_points.notes: NC fishing license required. Check NCWRC for delayed harvest and catch-and-release sections. Waders essential — water stays around 50°F year-round.

rivers.name: French Broad River
rivers.description: A beloved river flowing from the NC mountains through Asheville and into Tennessee. The Hot Springs section offers scenic Class II-III paddling through a stunning gorge with natural hot springs nearby.
rivers.put_in: Hot Springs River Access, Hot Springs NC

access_points.name: Hot Springs Put-In
access_points.description: Main put-in at the town of Hot Springs. Easy river access with parking adjacent to the Appalachian Trail crossing.
access_points.notes: The gorge section upstream offers Class II-III. Below Hot Springs mellows to Class I-II toward TN.

access_points.name: Stackhouse Take-Out
access_points.description: Take-out at Stackhouse, NC. Ends the classic Hot Springs gorge run. Roadside access off US-25.
access_points.notes: About 5 miles from the Hot Springs put-in. Shuttle required.

access_points.name: Hot Springs Riverside Fishing
access_points.description: Excellent smallmouth bass and musky fishing along the Hot Springs section. Deeper pools hold big fish year-round.
access_points.notes: NC fishing license required. Check NCWRC for regulations. Wading is productive in summer low water.

access_points.name: Hot Springs Natural Pools
access_points.description: Natural geothermal hot springs along the riverbank managed by Hot Springs Resort. A unique soaking experience right on the river.
access_points.notes: Fee required for resort pools. Some free natural seeps accessible from the river. Open year-round.

rivers.name: Pigeon River
rivers.description: The Pigeon River Gorge is one of the Southeast's premier whitewater runs. Consistent Class III-IV rapids through a spectacular gorge on the NC/TN border, fed by controlled releases from the Waterville power plant.

access_points.name: Waterville Put-In
access_points.description: Primary put-in for the Gorge run below the Waterville power plant. Large gravel bar with easy river access.
access_points.notes: Flow depends on power plant releases — check levels before launching. Outfitters in Hartford offer shuttles.

access_points.name: Hartford Take-Out
access_points.description: Main take-out at Hartford, TN near the I-40 exit. Multiple outfitters and parking along the river.
access_points.notes: About 5 miles of Class III-IV whitewater from Waterville. Commercial rafting operations run daily in season.

access_points.name: Pigeon River Gorge Fishing
access_points.description: Good rainbow and brown trout fishing in the tailwater section below the power plant. Cold, clear water holds fish year-round.
access_points.notes: NC/TN state line runs through this section — check which license applies. Wading can be dangerous during releases.

rivers.name: Hiwassee River
rivers.description: A beautiful dam-release river flowing through Cherokee National Forest in Tennessee. Known for excellent trout fishing and scenic flatwater paddling below Apalachia Dam, with the Hiwassee Scenic Railroad running alongside.

access_points.name: Reliance Put-In
access_points.description: Primary put-in at Reliance, TN. Large USFS parking area with restrooms and easy river access.
access_points.notes: Flow is dam-controlled — check TVA releases before launching. Most popular section runs to Patty Bridge.

access_points.name: Patty Bridge Take-Out
access_points.description: Popular take-out at Patty Bridge. Ends the classic Reliance to Patty Bridge run through Cherokee National Forest.
access_points.notes: About 5 miles from Reliance. Shuttle required. The Hiwassee Scenic Railroad runs alongside this section.

access_points.name: Hiwassee Tailwater Trout Fishing
access_points.description: World-class tailwater trout fishery below Apalachia Dam. Consistent cold water supports large wild brown and rainbow trout year-round.
access_points.notes: TN fishing license required. Check TWRA for catch-and-release sections. Wading can be hazardous during releases — watch for TVA warnings.

rivers.name: Ocoee River
rivers.description: The Middle Ocoee is one of America's most famous whitewater runs — non-stop Class III-IV action that hosted the 1996 Olympic whitewater events. TVA-controlled releases through the Ocoee Gorge deliver consistent, exciting rapids.

access_points.name: Ocoee Whitewater Center Put-In
access_points.description: The Olympic Whitewater Center built for the 1996 Games. Official put-in for the Middle Ocoee run. Large parking area with full facilities.
access_points.notes: Water runs when TVA releases from the flume — typically weekends and holidays in season. Check TVA schedule before going.

access_points.name: Ocoee No. 2 Take-Out
access_points.description: Take-out at the Ocoee No. 2 powerhouse. Ends the classic 5-mile Middle Ocoee run.
access_points.notes: About 5 miles of continuous Class III-IV. Dozens of commercial outfitters operate on the Ocoee — shuttle services widely available.

access_points.name: Upper Ocoee Olympic Course
access_points.description: The constructed Olympic slalom course used in the 1996 Atlanta Games. Still used for competition and training. Artificial course adjacent to the natural river.
access_points.notes: Check USFS for public access and event schedules. Competition events may restrict public use.
